text:
How may I help you?
Hi. Yeah, I'm here because over the past, like, couple months, I've been losing a lot of weight, but I didn't really have any other symptoms. But since, like, two weeks ago, I've been coughing up some blood, and now I have a fever as well.
Okay. So, you mentioned you noticed the weight loss first. How much weight have you lost roughly?
I've lost about 30 pounds in the past, like, eight months.
Okay. And have you been trying to lose weight?
No.
Okay. And, like, any changes to your diet or exercise?
No, nothing like that.
Okay. And more recently, you started to have a cough. When did that start?
The cough started two weeks ago.
Okay. And prior to this, did you have a cough?
No.
No. And are you bringing up any sputum or phlegm?
Yeah. Yeah, there's sputum for sure. And it looks bloody.
Okay. So, you're coughing up blood as well?
Yes, that's right.
Okay. And that's been going on for the last two weeks too?
Yes.
Okay. And how much blood have you been bringing up, roughly? If you can think of it in terms of tablespoons or teaspoons?
Like, every time I cough, it's about the size of a teaspoon.
Okay. And are there any triggers for your cough that you can think of?
I don't think so. They just happen throughout the day.
Okay. And have you tried anything for the cough?
I've tried those cough suppressants. I tried, like, salt water rinses. Just, like, at-home remedies and over-the-counter products, but nothing's helping.
Okay. And so, have you had anything, like, viral infectious type symptoms, such as, like, eye discharge or red eyes or a runny nose or even a sore throat?
No, nothing like that.
Okay. And have you had any headaches?
No, no headaches.
Okay. Have you had any runny nose?
No.
Okay. How about any, have you had a runny nose?
No, not since I moved here.
Okay. When did you move here and where did you move from?
I moved here a year back from Sri Lanka.
Okay. And in Sri Lanka, were any of your family members or people that you spent time around ever diagnosed with tuberculosis?
I mean, it's pretty common in Sri Lanka. None of my immediate family members were.
Okay. And do you smoke cigarettes?
No.
Do you smoke cannabis or use any cannabis products?
No.
Okay. And do you drink alcohol?
I don't.
Okay. How about the use of any other recreational drugs?
No.
Okay. And any family history of heart or lung problems?
Heart or lung? No, not that I know of.
Okay. And how about any cancers in the family?
No, no cancers.
I see. All right. Did you have any other questions for me today? I think that was everything I wanted to ask about for now.
No. What do you think I have and how do you think I should treat it?
So, yeah, it's sounding like it could be tuberculosis based on the kind of timeline of your symptoms and so on and cough and hemoptysis. And so we'll do a test for that, like an acid fast test of the sputum and get a chest x-ray. And if it is tuberculosis, it will require a prolonged course of antibiotics. So that will be over several months. And so we'll get the infectious disease team involved to help kind of guide our management with the appropriate antibiotic choice if it does come back to be tuberculosis.
Okay. Okay, that sounds good. Thank you.
